description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker:latest image with a docker:dind service and DOCKER_DRIVER=overlay2. Define stages build, release, and deploy, and a before_script that runs docker info, logs into the GitLab registry, installs nodejs, npm and the Heroku CLI via npm. In the build stage add a build job that pulls $CACHE_IMAGE or $RC_IMAGE, builds $BUILD_IMAGE with --pull and --cache-from $CACHE_IMAGE and $RC_IMAGE, tags it, and pushes it, using variables BUILD_IMAGE, CACHE_IMAGE, RC_IMAGE. In the release stage add three jobs: cache-image (runs on all branches except tags and master), rc-image (runs only on master), and versioned-image (runs only on tags); each pulls $BASE_TAG image, retags it to $RELEASE_TAG, and pushes it, using the shared script that runs docker image pull "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$BASE_TAG", docker image tag "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$BASE_TAG" "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$RELEASE_TAG", and docker image push "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$RELEASE_TAG" with appropriate variables. In the deploy stage add staging-app (runs on master, manual) and production-app (runs on tags, manual) jobs that log into the Heroku registry, pull the image $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHA, tag it as $HEROKU_REGISTRY/$HEROKU_APP_NAME/web, push it, and release the container to Heroku with heroku container:release web -a iris-system, setting HEROKU_APP_NAME to the respective staging or production app name.
